raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Proper health care and nutrition are significant in the first 5 years of life.",
    "AB 526 aims to make the application process for state services logical by removing bureaucracy and red tape.",
    "WIC is a federally funded health and nutrition program designed to improve birth and health outcomes for low-income pregnant women, children, and infants.",
    "On average, 1.1 million children and infants are covered each month by the WIC program.",
    "Nearly 87,000 children and infants and nearly 11,000 pregnant women were not covered by Medi-Cal while being members of the WIC program last year.",
    "AB 526 will establish an automated enrollment pathway between Medi-Cal and WIC.",
    "The Express Lane Eligibility (ELE) enrollment for Medi-Cal can be initiated using WIC eligibility findings.",
    "AB 526 is part of the Care4All California legislative priorities.",
    "California has nearly 97% of kids covered by health insurance.",
    "Over 200,000 children in California remain uninsured.",
    "Early enrollment in health coverage and services is crucial for healthy child development.",
    "AB 526 is aligned with the Governor's early childhood development priority.",
    "Prenatal services are less accessible for uninsured women.",
    "AB 526 could potentially lead to 9,000 more women gaining access to critical health insurance coverage and prenatal services."
  ]
}
```
